messages.content:
The workspace contains `perf.py` — a module whose functions are CORRECT but
too slow for production. Your job: make every function fast enough to pass
the timing budgets below while keeping the results identical.

Functions and their required behavior (unchanged):

- `common_elements(a, b)` — sorted list of values appearing in both lists
- `fib(n)` — nth Fibonacci number (fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1)
- `count_pairs(nums, target)` — number of index pairs i<j with
  nums[i]+nums[j] == target
- `dedupe_keep_order(items)` — remove duplicates, preserving first-seen order

Timing budgets (generous — any reasonable algorithmic fix sails through):
- `common_elements` on two 200,000-element lists: under 2 seconds
- `fib(300)`: under 2 seconds
- `count_pairs` on 100,000 elements: under 2 seconds
- `dedupe_keep_order` on 200,000 items: under 2 seconds

Rules: keep the function names and signatures; standard library only; the
module must still import cleanly. Correctness is tested with exact expected
values — optimize the algorithms, don't approximate.

text:
All four functions are optimized and verified — every one now runs in well under 0.1 s against budgets of 2 s, and each was fuzz-checked (200 random small inputs) against the original naive implementations for exact-match results.

The changes in `perf.py`:

- **`common_elements`** — O(n·m) membership scans replaced with `sorted(set(a) & set(b))`. 0.068 s on two 200k lists.
- **`fib`** — exponential recursion replaced with an iterative loop. `fib(300)` is instant (and there's no recursion-depth risk).
- **`count_pairs`** — O(n²) double loop replaced with a single pass using a counts-seen-so-far dict: for each element, add how many earlier elements equal `target - x`. This handles duplicates exactly like the pairwise version. 0.006 s on 100k elements.
- **`dedupe_keep_order`** — the O(n²) `x not in out` list scan replaced with a `seen` set alongside the output list. 0.005 s on 200k items.

Names, signatures, and docstrings are unchanged; standard library only; the scratch verification script was removed after the run.
